tossups.question: A sculptor struggles to make a canal-side monument to this film’s protagonists inoffensive in a Radu Jude (“RAH-doo ZHOO-day”) short film. This film’s Berlin cut introduced Edmund Meisel’s score. In this film, a man shouts “kill the Jews” before being beaten by a crowd gathered outside of a tent. This film cuts between shots of a wild-haired priest bouncing a cross on his hand and a group of soldiers aiming at other soldiers covered by a tarpaulin, who’d refused to say they liked the (*) borscht they were given. A woman holding a trampled baby faces down a line of soldiers in a scene from this film which exemplifies montage theory; in that scene, soldiers massacre civilians as a baby carriage rolls down the Odessa steps. For 10 points, name this Sergei Eisenstein film that fictionalizes a mutiny aboard the title vessel.
tossups.question_sanitized: A sculptor struggles to make a canal-side monument to this film's protagonists inoffensive in a Radu Jude ("RAH-doo ZHOO-day") short film. This film's Berlin cut introduced Edmund Meisel's score. In this film, a man shouts "kill the Jews" before being beaten by a crowd gathered outside of a tent. This film cuts between shots of a wild-haired priest bouncing a cross on his hand and a group of soldiers aiming at other soldiers covered by a tarpaulin, who'd refused to say they liked the (*) borscht they were given. A woman holding a trampled baby faces down a line of soldiers in a scene from this film which exemplifies montage theory; in that scene, soldiers massacre civilians as a baby carriage rolls down the Odessa steps. For 10 points, name this Sergei Eisenstein film that fictionalizes a mutiny aboard the title vessel.
tossups.answer: Battleship Potemkin [or Bronenosets Potyomkin] (The Radu Jude short is The Potemkinists)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Battleship Potemkin [or Bronenosets Potyomkin] (The Radu Jude short is The Potemkinists)

tossups.question: Terry Gilliam’s Brazil pays homage to this film sequence as Sam and Tuttle escape from the Ministry, replacing a key object from this scene with a vacuum cleaner. At its end, three separate shots of lion statues create the illusion that the lions are rising in response to the action of this sequence, which begins after the caption “suddenly . . .” interrupts a shot of a waving crowd that includes a legless man. The train station shootout from Brian de Palma’s The Untouchables draws from this scene, which uses montage to intercut close-ups of a screaming, bleeding woman wearing shattered glasses with shots of a rolling baby carriage. For 10 points, what sequence from Sergei Eisenstein’s Battleship Potemkin shows soldiers massacring a crowd fleeing down a namesake structure?
tossups.answer: Odessa steps scene [or the steps scene from Battleship Potemkin; accept answers mentioning staircase or stairs in place of “steps”; accept steps or stairs scene after “Battleship”; prompt on partial answers]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Odessa steps scene [or the steps scene from Battleship Potemkin; accept answers mentioning staircase or stairs in place of "steps"; accept steps or stairs scene after "Battleship"; prompt on partial answers]

tossups.question: Chris Marker’s A Grin Without a Cat opens with a montage of shots from this film and found protest footage. A priest in this film thumps his cross into his palm in a scene where the doctor Smirnov is killed. The only color in this film is on a red flag. (*) Vakulinchuk is left in a tent on a pier after dying in this film’s central conflict caused by maggot-covered meat. A woman with a pince-nez is among the civilians massacred in a montage where a baby carriage rolls down some steps in Odessa. For 10 points, name this film about a naval mutiny, directed by Sergei Eisenstein.
tossups.question_sanitized: Chris Marker's A Grin Without a Cat opens with a montage of shots from this film and found protest footage. A priest in this film thumps his cross into his palm in a scene where the doctor Smirnov is killed. The only color in this film is on a red flag. (*) Vakulinchuk is left in a tent on a pier after dying in this film's central conflict caused by maggot-covered meat. A woman with a pince-nez is among the civilians massacred in a montage where a baby carriage rolls down some steps in Odessa. For 10 points, name this film about a naval mutiny, directed by Sergei Eisenstein.

tossups.question: One character in this movie yells "Get out of the way, you sorceror!," as he shoves a priest out of the way. Protographed by the cinematographer Eduard Tissé, one scene in this movie features a legless man crawling alongside a retreating mob, whose members include a woman wearing a soon-to-be-broken pair of pince-nez. An explosion in this film appears to cause a stone lion to jump up, and its final shot shows this film's title object charging in at the (*) viewer and appearing to split the screen in half. A baby carriage rolls down some stairs in one part of this work, whose central action is touched off by maggot-infested meat. Its best-known scene includes a mother carrying her wounded child back to the troops that shot him. Based on a pre-communist uprising and featuring a montage filmed at a landmark in Odessa, for ten points, name this Sergei Eisenstein film.
tossups.question_sanitized: One character in this movie yells "Get out of the way, you sorceror!," as he shoves a priest out of the way. Protographed by the cinematographer Eduard Tisse, one scene in this movie features a legless man crawling alongside a retreating mob, whose members include a woman wearing a soon-to-be-broken pair of pince-nez. An explosion in this film appears to cause a stone lion to jump up, and its final shot shows this film's title object charging in at the (*) viewer and appearing to split the screen in half. A baby carriage rolls down some stairs in one part of this work, whose central action is touched off by maggot-infested meat. Its best-known scene includes a mother carrying her wounded child back to the troops that shot him. Based on a pre-communist uprising and featuring a montage filmed at a landmark in Odessa, for ten points, name this Sergei Eisenstein film.

bonuses.leadin: One of this artist’s paintings depicts the 127-kilogram woman Sue Tilley lying naked on a couch. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist who sits on a wooden chair against an orange background in a triptych by a different artist containing “three studies” of him.
This artist painted Freud, who was both his friend and rival, in Three Studies of Lucien Freud. This Irish artist also created Three Studies for the Base of a Crucifixion and several screaming popes.
Many of Bacon’s works were influenced by this film, such as a shot that depicts a nurse whose broken glasses pierce her bleeding eye. A scene from this film is set on the Odessa Steps.
answers: Lucien Freud (The first painting is titled Benefits Supervisor Sleeping.)
answers_sanitized: Lucien Freud (The first painting is titled Benefits Supervisor Sleeping.)

bonuses.leadin: The Union Station shootout in The Untouchables was inspired by a scene from another film set in this city. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this city, the site of a 1925 film sequence in which soldiers descend an endless flight of steps to attack a crowd, including a mother pushing a baby carriage and a nurse with broken glasses.
The Odessa Steps sequence appears in this Sergei Eisenstein film that documents a 1905 naval mutiny aboard the title ship.
Eisenstein was a director from this country, whose other filmmakers include Andrei Tarkovsky.
answers: the Soviet Union [or the U.S.S.R.; or the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics; or Russia; or Russian Federation]
answers_sanitized: the Soviet Union [or the U.S.S.R.; or the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics; or Russia; or Russian Federation]

Eisenstein was a director from this country, whose other filmmakers include Andrei Tarkovsky.

bonuses.leadin: In a triptych with three different views of the same scene, this man painted his friend Lucian Freud sitting on a chair while encased in a distorted cubic wire frame. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Irish painter who painted orange background behind twisted gray beings representing the Furies in Three Studies for Figures at the Base of a Crucifixion. He also painted Screaming Pope.

In another triptych, this lover of Bacon sits on a toilet seat in one panel and vomits into a sink in another. This man's overdose on barbiturates devastated Bacon in the early 1970s, leading to the creation of the Black Triptychs.

Bacon painted a study of a shot from this film showing broken glasses hanging from the nose of a nurse who is bleeding from her eye. In another shot from this film, a baby carriage falls down the Odessa steps.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about everyone’s favorite film...Soviet film, FTPE.
parts: Blood pours down a screaming woman’s face and a baby carriage rolls in the “Odessa Steps” section of this 1925 film, made a year after its director’s film, Strike.

This female Ukranian director made a film about a female fighter pilot entitled Wings and won the Golden Bear for a film about Belarussian partisans in World War II titled The Ascent. She was killed in a car accident in 1979.

This American film borrowed many shots from Czech director Jindrich Polak’s Ikarie XB-1. Special effects supervisor Douglas Trumbull used slit-scan photography for a scene in this film where multicolored light emerges from a vanishing point. The antagonist of this film sings “Daisy Bell” before dying.
